summary:
Oligomenorrhea or irregular bleeding are common findings associated with Asherman’s syndrome; this condition involves intrauterine adhesions causing disruptions in normal menstruation cycles. The primary hallmark is an alteration in menstrual pattern reflecting underlying structural damage inside the uterus. \boxed{Oligomenorrhea}

The summary captures both aspects mentioned by Agent2 while condensing it into two concise statements that highlight critical elements related directly back to patient symptoms indicative of Asherman Syndrome. It focuses specifically on how alterations in menstrual cycle characteristics serve diagnostic markers for identifying cases where such conditions may be present clinically speaking.
